---
title: "About"
page-layout: full
toc: false
number-sections: false
---

## Mission

This project is building a public math site for people who want a stronger research foundation in `CS`, `AI / ML`, and `engineering`.

The long-term goal is not just to summarize content. It is to make mathematical ideas readable, useful, and connected to real research practice.

## Design Principles

- Organize by `topic and use`, not by textbook.
- Keep `proofs, applications, and papers` connected.
- Make every serious page readable as a standalone entry point.
- Distinguish `stable core math` from `moving research frontier`.
- Treat publication guidance as an advanced workflow layer, not the site's identity.

## What The Site Is Trying To Be

The site is meant to work as three things at once:

- a dependable topic-first math reference
- a bridge into applications, paper reading, and research workflow
- a public long-term knowledge system rather than a one-off note dump

The structure is already broad enough to support real study and paper reading. The remaining work is mostly polish, validation, and continued depth rather than inventing a new site model.
